content.purpose: Manage and sell US and Canadian franchises for Papa Johns restaurants
content.parent_relationship: PJI is sole member with full ownership interest

content.fiscal_year: Ends last Sunday in December each year
content.comprehensive_income: No other comprehensive income; equals net income

cash_and_equivalents.policy: Highly liquid investments with initial maturity ≤3 months
cash_and_equivalents.actual: Zero cash — all swept to PJUSA for centralized cash management

accounts_receivable.credit_extension: Based on franchisee financial condition, no collateral required

content.income_taxes: PJF is a single member LLC, disregarded entity for federal and state tax. All income/loss on PJI's consolidated return.

revenue_recognition.royalties: Recognized as sales occur, based on percentage of franchise restaurant sales. Standard rate 5%. Incentive reductions recognized at same time.
revenue_recognition.franchise_fees: Billed at store opening. Combined with franchise right (not separately distinguishable). Amortized straight-line from opening through 10-year FA term.
revenue_recognition.area_development_fees: Billed at execution of development agreement. Included in deferred revenue. Allocated pro-rata to stores opened under that development agreement.
revenue_recognition.renewal_fees: Billed before renewal date. Amortized over renewal period.
revenue_recognition.incentive_programs: Royalty incentives, development incentives, and other support initiatives reduce royalties and franchise fees.

refranchising_royalty_streams.recognition: Over 10-year franchise agreement terms executed concurrent with dispositions

content.fair_value: Assets and liabilities approximate carrying value due to short-term nature

content.pji_relationship: Parent company, directly or indirectly, of all Papa Johns entities. Transferred franchise agreements to PJF on Feb 22, 2021.

franchise_supervision_agreement.services: Financial, support, training, supervision, compliance-related services
franchise_supervision_agreement.note: Updated internal cost allocations in 2024 resulted in higher fees

promissory_note.due_date: Earlier of written demand or December 31, 2030
promissory_note.default_penalty_rate: 10.25% annual, commencing only on due date if in default

content.advances_to_related_parties: Excess cash flows above distributions to PJI, parked at PJUSA under centralized cash management

content.distribution_mechanism: Forgiveness or extinguishment of Advances to related parties initially made through centralized cash management, subsequently settled through Member's Equity
